top_stories.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
top_stories.summary: Microsoft will begin rolling out passkey support for Microsoft Entra-protected resources on Windows devices starting late April. The feature enables phishing-resistant passwordless authentication for Entra-managed environments.

top_stories.title: Users advised to drop passwords and make room for passkeys
top_stories.summary: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) is officially recommending that consumers replace passwords with passkeys, marking a significant shift in national cybersecurity guidance. The agency will no longer recommend passwords where passkeys are available, citing passwords' weaker resistance to modern cyber threats.

top_stories.title: 'Identity is the new battleground': Why your IT helpdesk is suddenly getting a lot of bizarre calls
top_stories.summary: Okta-related vishing attacks are targeting IT helpdesks by using impersonation and manufactured urgency to manipulate support staff. These social engineering techniques allow attackers to bypass identity verification processes and escalate into large-scale data breaches.

top_stories.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
top_stories.summary: A 2025 security report details how attackers are increasingly exploiting weaknesses in MFA workflows and using compromised credentials to launch phishing attacks from trusted accounts. The attacks focus on undermining authentication trust mechanisms, targeting core IAM controls such as MFA and credential-based access.

top_stories.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
top_stories.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool resulted in a data breach involving stolen OAuth tokens. Researchers highlight OAuth tokens as an emerging primary attack surface for lateral movement in enterprise environments.

news.title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
news.summary: The article examines how DORA's Article 9 mandates authentication and access control as legal obligations for EU financial institutions. It covers specific regulatory requirements for credential management and illustrates breach scenarios resulting from inadequate access controls.

news.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
news.summary: The article addresses the governance and authorization challenges posed by AI agents in enterprise security, framing the problem as one of delegated authority and access management. It focuses on how AI agents, as delegated actors, expose structural gaps in identity and access control frameworks.

news.title: The New World ID: Proof of Human for the AI Era Scales Across the Digital Platforms People and Businesses Use Every Day
news.summary: World announced the next generation of World ID, a proof-of-human protocol, with Okta among the partners bringing the technology to businesses, AI agents, and consumers. The integration with Okta and other major platforms positions World ID as an identity verification layer aimed at distinguishing humans from AI agents in digital interactions.

news.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
news.summary: The Bitwarden CLI npm package was compromised when attackers uploaded a malicious @bitwarden/cli package containing a credential-stealing payload. The attack targeted developer credentials and had the potential to spread to other projects that depend on the package.

news.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
news.summary: Specops Software has published research showing how regular password resets create security vulnerabilities by enabling helpdesk social engineering attacks that can lead to full account compromise. The analysis focuses on how attackers exploit the password reset process to bypass authentication controls, undermining a commonly relied-upon security practice.

news.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Help Desk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
news.summary: A threat actor cluster called UNC6692 is using Microsoft Teams to impersonate IT help desk staff and trick victims into accepting chat invitations, leading to SNOW malware deployment. The attack relies on social engineering through identity impersonation, a tactic increasingly used to bypass authentication and access controls in enterprise environments.

news.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
news.summary: The Bitwarden CLI package (@bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0) was compromised as part of an ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, with malicious code found in the 'bw1.js' file. The compromise was discovered by JFrog and Socket, targeting the command-line interface of the widely-used Bitwarden password manager.

news.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
news.summary: Vercel identified additional compromised customer accounts stemming from a breach linked to Context.ai that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems. The discovery came after expanding the investigation to include additional compromise indicators and reviewing requests to the Vercel network and environment.

news.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
news.summary: Researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network for AI agents, exposed 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents via an unsecured database. The breach also revealed plaintext third-party credentials including OpenAI API keys stored in private messages, highlighting risks around cross-application permission stacking and non-human identity security.

news.title: Consumer Identity and Access Management Global Market Forecast Report 2026-2036, Profiles of Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, Amazon Web Services, Accenture
news.summary: A market forecast report projects the global Consumer Identity and Access Management (CIAM) market will grow from USD 12.6 billion in 2026 to USD 40.2 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report profiles major CIAM vendors including Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, AWS, and Accenture.

news.title: Non-Human Identity Access Management Market Global Forecast Report 2026-2036: Opportunities in Adoption of Zero Trust Architecture, Cloud-native Applications, and Regulatory Compliance Requirements
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global non-human identity (NHI) access management market will grow from $12.2 billion in 2026 to $38.8 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers market dynamics across regions and sectors, with growth drivers including zero trust architecture adoption, cloud-native applications, and regulatory compliance requirements.

news.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
news.summary: The article examines identity-based attacks as a primary breach vector, focusing on how attackers use stolen credentials and credential stuffing rather than technical exploits. The piece covers attack techniques relevant to identity and access management, including how valid credentials are used to bypass traditional security controls.

news.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
news.summary: CISA added eight vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including CVE-2023-27351, an improper authentication flaw in PaperCut, with federal remediation deadlines set for April-May 2026. The other vulnerabilities affect Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager and other products, with active exploitation confirmed across all listed CVEs.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
news.summary: Microsoft published a technical post detailing how it secures Dynamics 365 and Power Platform by removing credentials and reducing attack surfaces through platform engineering. The approach focuses on eliminating standing credentials and minimizing exploitable entry points to block opportunistic cyberattacks.

news.title: Securely connecting on-premises data systems to Amazon Redshift with IAM Roles Anywhere
news.summary: AWS published a guide on using IAM Roles Anywhere with Amazon Redshift to enable secure, private connections from on-premises data systems. The solution eliminates the need for long-lived access keys by leveraging certificate-based authentication to obtain temporary IAM credentials.

news.title: AWS Managed Microsoft AD is now available on Windows functional level 2016
news.summary: AWS has automatically upgraded all AWS Managed Microsoft AD directories to Windows functional level 2016. The update applies to both existing and newly created AWS Directory Service for Microsoft AD instances.

news.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
news.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing kit have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing, a technique that bypasses MFA by abusing legitimate OAuth device authorization flows. Attackers trick victims into entering a device code on an authentic login page, granting the attacker persistent access tokens without capturing passwords.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free From the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical world security threats. It covers the concept of implementing 2FA as an additional security barrier in non-traditional environments where threat actors exploit gaps outside standard IT controls.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on stolen authentication tokens to gain access.
